## 🔗 Union Logic (Client-Side)

Since FHIR has no native cross-resource `UNION`, collect `subject` / `patient` references from each result set and merge them:

```python
hcc_pts    = {r["subject"]["reference"] for r in condition_results}
ct_pts     = {r["subject"]["reference"] for r in procedure_results}
afp_pts    = {r["subject"]["reference"] for r in observation_results}

union_pts  = hcc_pts | ct_pts | afp_pts   # all patients appearing in ANY arm
intersection = hcc_pts & ct_pts & afp_pts # patients meeting ALL three criteria
```

**Clinical interpretation of each set:**
- **Union** → any patient with *either* an HCC diagnosis, *or* a CT abdomen procedure, *or* an AFP lab result (broad surveillance/workup cohort)
- **Intersection** → patients with all three (highest specificity for confirmed HCC workup)
- **HCC-only** → patients diagnosed but possibly missing imaging/labs (chart-only dx)
